reasoning: Ariarne Titmus holds the fully-ratified women's 200m freestyle long course world record (1:52.23), set in June 2024. As of the October 26, 2025 knowledge cutoff, no swimmer has broken this mark. The late-year swimming calendar from November to January primarily focuses on short-course (25m) events, making long-course world records highly unlikely. Even the few long-course meets scheduled for December or January (like the Toyota US Open and Queensland Championships) are rarely targeted for full tapers or peak performance by Titmus or her main rivals (Mollie O'Callaghan and Summer McIntosh). Historically, swimmers are several seconds off world-record pace during these off-season domestic competitions. Therefore, the chance of the record falling increases very slightly over the three-month horizon but remains fundamentally low.
